content.boundary: Applies to resource allocation and capacity-building for non-selective comprehensive schools nationally; does not change gimnázium admissions ages or criteria in this phase.
content.change_lever: Redirect capacity-building resources (teacher recruitment/retention incentives, facility investment, curriculum support) toward comprehensive schools, particularly in rural and disadvantaged areas, rather than altering the selective-track structure directly.
content.name: Preserve tracks, expand comprehensive-school quality investment (compensatory strategy)
content.system_problem: Families seek selective tracks partly due to distrust in local school quality; structural selection cannot be eliminated without first addressing underlying disparities in school quality and teacher supply, creating a sequencing precondition for any reform [F-live-implementation_planning-07].
